title: AI — Consumption & Cost
description: Token throughput and spend — the FinOps view of an LLM workload. THIS DASHBOARD IS CURRENTLY EMPTY BY DESIGN: the app computes promptTokens/completionTokens/computedCost and writes them to Postgres, but never exposes them as metrics (#104). Cost data is also unreliable until LiteLLM meters the calls (#102), because LibreChat's Agents API returns an all-zero usage object (#38).

options.content:
**Two prerequisites, both tracked:**

1. **#104 — expose token/cost metrics.** `processor.ts` already computes `promptTokens`, `completionTokens` and `computedCost` and writes them to Postgres, but no Prometheus counter exposes them. Needed: `nexus_scheduler_run_tokens_total{type,model}` and `nexus_scheduler_run_cost_total{model}`.
2. **#102 — LiteLLM as the model gateway.** LibreChat's Agents API returns an **all-zero usage object** (#38), so token counts are frequently `null` at the source. LiteLLM meters every call and also exposes its own per-key/model spend metrics.

Until both land, spend can only be answered with SQL against the `runs` table — not alerted on.

panels.title: Tokens/sec by model
panels.description: Requires a `model` label. Label by model — never by agentId/jobId/userId (unbounded cardinality); per-user breakdowns belong in the audit trail.

panels.title: Spend (24h)
panels.description: Requires nexus_scheduler_run_cost_total. Meaningful only once LiteLLM (#102) makes usage authoritative.

panels.title: Cost per successful run
panels.description: Unit economics: spend divided by successful runs. The number that tells you whether a model/prompt change actually paid off.

panels.title: LiteLLM spend by key/model
panels.description: LiteLLM exposes native Prometheus metrics for per-key/model spend, tokens, latency and failures — the authoritative source once #102 lands. It also enforces hard budgets, turning this from a cost *report* into a cost *control*.
